metadata.title:
EMPLOYMENT


MAN REPLIES

PLAINS REASON FOR THE IN
CREASE FEE CHARGED FOR
SUPPLYING WORK.

pages.raw_text:
The employment agent has rea-
son for increasing the price ne asks
for placing men in work, according
to the explanation of present condi-
tons made by G. e. Carr. Of the
Carr employment agency, in reply
to a complaint made by applicants
who charge the agents with holdup
tactics. In the first place, he
points ont the employment office
license has been raised in the past
six months from $50 to 5000 per
year, the highest license fee i'm
posed on the business in the West.
Seattle and San Francisco each
charge 800 and in many instances
the license Is paid quartedly where
1t must be paid in lump sum
here.


An item of more Importance than
this is the fact that the agents are
now compelled to spend money and
time in locating work for possible
customers in order to continue in
business. Carr says he recently
spent $50 in one week running
around the country In search Of
places where men were needed. The
old days of sitting in the Office with
